topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting economic development and housing accessibility in specific communities and regions.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to tax-exempt enterprise zone facility bonds and empowerment zones
extend the period of designation for empowerment zones through December 31, 2016
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to determine for each county with a population exceeding one million
to increase the limitation on the maximum original principal obligation of a mortgage that may be purchased by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac

topic.name: Disaster Management and Recovery
topic.description: Legislation focused on federal and state responses to natural disasters, including flood mitigation and emergency resource restoration.

Document 1: Authorizes the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to make grants to local governments for flood mitigation projects.
Projects with significant capacities for job creation.
Document 3: Allows state agencies and political subdivisions... to take all reasonable and necessary means to access, reconstruct, repair, or restore such sources, supplies, or infrastructure.
Prohibits federal agencies from interfering... during a declared state of emergency.

topic.name: Government Transparency and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and disclosure within federal government operations and activities.

Document 1: Amends the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) to revise requirements for federal agency disclosures of information requested under that Act.
Directs the Attorney General to notify the Special Counsel of civil actions taken for arbitrary and capricious rejections of requests for agency records.
Document 2: Establishes the Department of Commerce and the Workforce, headed by a Secretary of Commerce and the Workforce, as an executive department for the advancement of economic growth and job opportunities.
Document 3: Amends the Ethics in Government Act of 1978 and the Rules of the House of Representatives to expand disclosure requirements for the personal financial information of Members of Congress.
Requires that, to the extent practicable, all bills, resolutions, orders, and votes be created, exchanged, and published in searchable electronic formats.

topic.name: Communications Act Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Communications Act of 1934 addressing internet access, telecommunications in correctional facilities, and broadcasting regulations.

Document 1: prohibit elementary schools, secondary schools, or libraries from blocking Internet access to LGBTQ resources
amends the Communications Act of 1934
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to authorize the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to permit the supervisory authority of a correctional facility to operate a jamming system
Requires the supervisory authority to file a notice of intent with the FCC
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow the renewal of a full-power analog television broadcasting license
Prohibits the FCC from extending or renewing a full-power television broadcast license that authorizes analog service

topic.name: Taxpayer Rights and Protections
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing taxpayer rights, regulating tax preparers, and imposing penalties for violations of tax-related laws.

Document 1: limits the penalty for failure to disclose a reportable transaction
requires the Commissioner of Internal Revenue to report on penalties relating to tax shelters
Document 2: increase criminal and civil penalties for unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer information
extend to two years the time limit for contesting a tax levy
Document 3: gives the Secretary of the Treasury the authority to regulate the practice of tax return preparers
sanction preparers for incompetency or misconduct

topic.name: Veteran Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing opportunities and support for veteran-owned small businesses through various programs and preferences.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to make small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans eligible for the award by the Small Business Administration (SBA) of small business development grants.
Eligibility for small business development grants in the same manner as small businesses owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.
Document 2: Establish within the Small Business Administration (SBA) an Office of Export Development and Promotion to promote small business goods and services abroad.
Develop performance measures to support Office export growth goals.
Document 3: Authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to give preference to offerors based on the percentage of the offeror's full-time employees who are veterans.
Any offeror determined to have willfully and intentionally misrepresented the veteran status of its employees shall be debarred from contracting with the VA for at least five years.

topic.name: Regulatory Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing safety regulations and accountability in various industries, particularly in transportation and mining.

Document 1: establish integrity verification requirements for gas or hazardous liquid pipeline facilities
require an operator of a pipeline facility that contains a pipeline segment without a documented pressure test to hydro-test or replace the pipeline segment
Document 2: prohibit the Office of Surface Mining and Reclamation Enforcement from accepting new self-bonds for coal reclamation
requires the Department of Interior to issue rules establishing limitations on surety bonds
Document 3: expands coverage to federal, state, and local government employees
increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA

topic.name: Post-9/11 Compensation and Unemployment Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to health compensation for 9/11 victims and unemployment benefits extensions in the aftermath of economic challenges.

Document 1: Extends the World Trade Center (WTC) Health Program Fund indefinitely
Removes the cap on payments under the September 11th Victim Compensation Fund of 2001.
Document 2: Amends the Social Security Act to require the Secretary of Labor to make unemployment compensation modernization incentive payments
Limits the use of transferred amounts to the payment of cash unemployment benefits to individuals.
Document 3: Extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals
Expresses the sense of Congress that the House of Representatives and the Senate should reconvene on or before December 18, 2013.

topic.name: Education Reform and Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at improving educational opportunities and funding mechanisms for various student populations, including charter schools and special education.

Document 1: Revises subpart 1 (Charter School Programs) of part B (Public Charter Schools)
Requires grantees to use 10% of the grant funds to provide technical assistance
Document 2: Amends the Scholarships for Opportunity and Results Act to require the Secretary of Education to use funds
Authorizes grants for the improvement of special education services
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states to provide Kindergarten Plus (K+) programs
Requires states, in awarding subgrants, to give priority to LEAs that serve the greatest number or percentage of eligible students

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: These documents discuss various legislative acts related to criminal justice and law enforcement practices.

doc_id_1: Prohibits states from receiving drug control funds
doc_id_2: A Child Is Missing Alert and Recovery Center Act
Grants for missing child recovery services
doc_id_3: District of Columbia Local Prosecutor Establishment Act
Shifts prosecution responsibility to local prosecutor's office

topic.name: Offshore Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at prohibiting and regulating oil and gas activities in specific areas of the Outer Continental Shelf to enhance coastal protection.

Document 1: prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from offering for oil and gas leasing
specified areas of the Eastern Gulf of Mexico
Document 2: suspend all activities relating to the development of a new or revised five-year plan
joint investigation by the Secretaries of the Interior and of Homeland Security into the Deepwater Horizon incident
Document 3: prohibit oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the Outer Continental Shelf located off the coast of California

topic.name: Environmental Restoration Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the restoration and protection of key ecosystems, specifically the Chesapeake Bay and Everglades, and the management of invasive species affecting wetlands.

Document 1: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1996 to eliminate the pilot program status of the Chesapeake Bay environmental restoration and protection program.

Directs the Secretary to establish a program, to be administered by the National Fish and Wildlife Foundation, to provide small watershed grants for technical and financial assistance to local governments and nonprofit organizations in the Chesapeake Bay region.
Document 2: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 2000 to authorize the Secretary of the Army... to carry out projects for: (1) the Caloosahatchee River (C-43) West Basin Storage Reservoir; (2) the Biscayne Bay coastal wetland.
any group of projects under the Plan that the Secretary determines will provide regional or watershed ecosystem or water supply benefits.
Document 3: Amends the Nutria Eradication and Control Act of 2003 to revise the nutria eradication program by authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to provide financial assistance to Delaware, Louisiana, Maryland, North Carolina, Oregon, Virginia, and Washington.
Requires the Secretary and the National Invasive Species Council to develop long-term nutria control or eradication programs to: (1) significantly reduce and restore nutria damaged wetlands.

topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting various tax deductions and credits.

Internal Revenue Code

Internal Revenue Code

tax credit for adoption expenses
